directive.title: Basic RFQ list and detail pages with static data

auditor.summary: The generated code is incomplete, with only 4 of 15 required files created. The existing files (types/rfq.ts, lib/mock-data/rfqs.ts, components/ui/ErrorBoundary.tsx, components/rfqs/RFQStatusBadge.tsx) are well-structured and follow TypeScript best practices with no 'any' types. The mock data contains 12 RFQ objects covering all 5 status types as required. However, the core page components (list page, detail page, loading states, error boundaries, table, detail card, requirements, timeline) are missing entirely. This results in 12 of 25 acceptance criteria being FAIL, 7 PARTIAL, and only 6 PASS. The build cannot function as a complete application without the missing files. The code that exists is of good quality and follows the specified patterns, but the truncation of generation left critical gaps that prevent the directive from being satisfied.

top_issues.description: Generated code is incomplete — only 4 of 15 required files were created. Missing: app/rfqs/page.tsx, app/rfqs/[id]/page.tsx, app/rfqs/loading.tsx, app/rfqs/[id]/loading.tsx, app/rfqs/error.tsx, app/rfqs/[id]/error.tsx, components/rfqs/RFQTable.tsx, components/rfqs/RFQDetailCard.tsx, components/rfqs/RFQRequirements.tsx, components/rfqs/RFQTimeline.tsx. Build cannot function without these files.
Error boundary has retry button but no back link to /rfqs as required by acceptance criterion 15 and constraints. Also missing ISO8601 timestamp logging as specified in audit_focus.

Component is missing 'use client' directive. While it's a pure presentational component, the build spec requires all client components to be explicitly marked. However, this component has no hooks or state, so it could remain a server component. The spec says 'RFQStatusBadge must be a pure presentational component with no internal state' which is satisfied.
